interval.label: Alarm Interval
interval.tooltip: Used to set the alarm frequency. For example, setting it to 5 seconds means that after an alarm is triggered by the algorithm under this camera, no further alarms will be generated within the next 5 seconds.

length.label: Alarm Window Length
length.tooltip: The judgment cycle for alarm events. For example, setting it to 5 times (seconds) means that the system needs to analyze for 5 consecutive seconds before deciding whether to trigger an alarm. If you want the alarm to be more sensitive, this value can be reduced, but it may lead to more false alarms.

threshold.label: Alarm Threshold
threshold.tooltip: Usually used together with the alarm window length. For example, if the alarm window length is set to 5 seconds and the alarm threshold is set to 3 seconds, it means that within the 5-second time window, if the algorithm detects 3 seconds of hits, an alarm will be triggered.

conf_thres.label: Human body segmentation confidence
conf_thres.tooltip: It refers to separating the human body region from the background or other objects in the image, while confidence represents the model's judgment of the accuracy or reliability of the segmentation result. High confidence indicates that the model is very confident about the detected human body region, believing that the segmentation result is highly accurate and closely aligned with the actual situation. Low confidence means that the model has less confidence in the segmentation result, and it may consider the result unreliable, with a potential risk of missegmentation or missed segmentation.
